lifecycle.operator_decision_summary: Operator 2026-07-02 ordered per-MT drift spot-checks with adversarial sub-agent review; no MT has been validated by an official validator. Wave-B audit: STALE PROOF at 3bf307a8 - tier3_palmistry_empty_state_and_accesskit_ids_present asserts contains('No freeze/crash records') but panel.rs:363 now renders 'No freeze/crash/child-stall records.' (changed by MT-106 commit 547b29c9); the substring does not match so the MT-087 proof test FAILS at the audited commit (test_survivor_forward.rs already uses the new string; test_diagnostics_panel.rs was not updated). Panel implementation itself is correct (pure projection, hosted in Settings per operator steer, reachable live, AccessKit ids match contract, corroborated by inspected PNG). Also the saved PNG shows Recent-events + Tier-3 sections below the scroll fold (AC-007-3 asks all sections legible) and the full-suite no-regression window never finished per implementation_result. Fix: update the assertion to the MT-106 text or a stable prefix, re-run test_diagnostics_panel, capture a scrolled/taller screenshot on the next proof pass.

scope.summary:
Goal: Build the in-app DIAGNOSTICS PANEL — a new egui pane that renders the live internal_diagnostics state (Master Spec v02.196 §5.8.4 'in-app Diagnostics Panel' + §10.12.5 three-tier model). The panel is a PROJECTION over internal_diagnostics (it holds NO state authority, §5.8.4): it reads `diagnostics::snapshot_last_n` (last-N events, MT-082), `frame_stats()` (MT-085), the resource counters + GpuInfo (MT-086), and the live heartbeat. It MUST be mounted as a REAL pane in the running app (follow the editor_pane_factories.rs host-mount pattern), with AccessKit metadata + a visual (wgpu screenshot) proof. (The Palmistry freeze/crash records the panel also surfaces per §10.12.5 are wired in MT-093; this MT renders the Tier 2 internal_diagnostics surface + leaves a clearly-typed empty section for the Tier 3 Palmistry records until MT-093 forwards them.)

Exact files to create/edit:
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/diagnostics/panel.rs` — `DiagnosticsPanel` (the egui widget) + `DiagnosticsPaneFactory` (impl `crate::pane_registry::PaneFactory`). Declare `pub mod panel;` in diagnostics/mod.rs.
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/pane_registry.rs` — add a `PaneType::Diagnostics` variant to the `PaneType` enum (line ~36), with its `label()` => "Diagnostics" and `default_label()` => "Diagnostics" arms (both match statements). (Note: a `VisualDebugger` variant already exists for the §10.12 visual-debugger surface; the new three-tier Diagnostics panel is a DISTINCT §10.12.5 surface — add a dedicated variant rather than overload VisualDebugger.)
- EDIT `src/frontend/handshake_native/src/app.rs` — register the real `DiagnosticsPaneFactory` in the factory map (in `build_default_factories` ~line 590 or alongside `install_editor_mounts` ~line 742), replacing the PlaceholderPaneFactory for `PaneType::Diagnostics`. Follow the EXACT pattern editor_pane_factories.rs uses: a thin factory whose `&self render` reads the process-global diagnostics snapshot each frame (no &mut needed — the data comes from the MT-082 global recorder, so no shared-cell is even required; the factory is stateless and reads the global).
- CREATE `src/frontend/handshake_native/tests/test_diagnostics_panel.rs`.

Panel content (panel.rs) — `PaneFactory::render(&self, ui, ctx)`:
- Heartbeat section: the live counter + time-since-last-beat (read from the ring header via the recorder, or an in-process mirror of the last bumped value) — shows the app is alive.
- Frame-time section: last/p50/p95 from `frame_stats()` (MT-085), formatted ms.
- Resource section: CPU% + RSS from the last ResourceSample + the GpuInfo hardware line (MT-086).
- Last-N events section: a scrolling list of the last ~50 DiagEvents (MT-082 snapshot) — render each as its typed fields (event_code label, phase, severity, counters, timestamp). NO free text (there is none — DiagEvent is typed integers; map event_code -> a static human label for display).
- A Tier-3 Palmistry section: a clearly-labeled placeholder/empty-state ('No freeze/crash records' / 'Palmistry forwarding wired in MT-093') so the §10.12.5 three-tier layout is present from this MT, honestly empty until MT-093.
- AccessKit: register the pane container as `Role::Region`/`Group` with a stable `author_id = "diagnostics_panel"` through the existing accessibility layer (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/registry.rs), and key child sections (e.g. `diagnostics_heartbeat`, `diagnostics_events`) so a no-context model + swarm agents can address it. NO perpetual spinner; theme tokens only (no Color32 literal outside theme/palette.rs — CONTROL-4).

Host-mount pattern (the REAL mount, not a widget harness): mirror MT-079's editor mount — the factory is registered in the app's factory HashMap keyed on PaneType::Diagnostics, and `PaneFactory::render` is CONSUMED by the live pane host each frame. Prove it renders in the LIVE app tree (a kittest driving the real HandshakeApp that opens/mounts the Diagnostics pane), not only as a standalone widget.

Reuse note: the PaneFactory trait + PaneRenderContext + the factory-map registration are the EXISTING WP-011/MT-079 pattern (editor_pane_factories.rs is the worked reference). The accessibility registry + theme layer are reused (registry.rs, theme/palette.rs, theme/syntax.rs). The diagnostics data all comes from the MT-082/005/006 process-global — the panel allocates none of its own authority. src/backend untouched.

Risk if missed: §5.8.4 + §10.12.5 unmet (no operator-facing consumer of internal_diagnostics); a panel built only as a widget (not host-mounted) would never render in the live app (the MT-079 RISK-2 'mounted but not consumed' class); a Color32 literal would break CONTROL-4.
acceptance_criteria: AC-007-1 (REAL MOUNT, prove first): the `DiagnosticsPaneFactory` is registered in app.rs (PlaceholderPaneFactory NO LONGER used for PaneType::Diagnostics) — proven by a kittest that drives the live HandshakeApp tree, mounts/opens the Diagnostics pane, and asserts the REAL panel AccessKit subtree renders (author_id='diagnostics_panel' Region with the heartbeat/events child nodes), NOT a placeholder node.

AC-007-2: the panel renders live internal_diagnostics state — after the live frame loop has run (heartbeat advancing, at least one DiagEvent recorded), the kittest asserts the panel shows a non-zero heartbeat, frame-time stats, and at least one event row, all sourced from the MT-082/005/006 globals (the panel holds no own authority).

AC-007-3: a wgpu screenshot of the mounted Diagnostics panel is saved to the EXTERNAL artifact root (Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test/wp-kernel-015-mt-007/) and is visually inspected by the coder to confirm the panel is readable (heartbeat, frame-time, resource, events sections legible; HBR-VIS).
AC-007-4: the Tier-3 Palmistry section renders an honest empty-state (no freeze/crash records yet; forwarding wired in MT-093) — the §10.12.5 three-tier layout is present, not faked.

AC-007-5: AccessKit nodes present (diagnostics_panel + section ids); no perpetual spinner; NO Color32 literal outside theme/palette.rs (CONTROL-4 green via test_theme / grep); the panel uses theme tokens only.

AC-007-6: `cargo build -p handshake-native` (lib + bins) clean; clippy `--all-targets -D warnings` clean; full suite `-j 2` green; external target dir; `git ls-files src/**/*.png` empty (screenshots external only).
proof_targets: PT-007-A: kittest drives the live HandshakeApp, mounts the Diagnostics pane, asserts the REAL panel AccessKit subtree (not placeholder) + saves a wgpu screenshot to the external root.
PT-007-B: kittest asserts the panel shows live heartbeat + frame-time + resource + >=1 event row from the globals.
PT-007-C: Tier-3 empty-state assertion + AccessKit ids present.
PT-007-D: CONTROL-4 (no Color32 outside theme) green; `cargo build --lib --bins` clean + clippy clean + full suite -j 2 green; git ls-files src/**/*.png empty.
implementation_notes: OPERATOR STEER 2026-06-27 - DIAGNOSTICS LIVES IN THE OPTIONS/SETTINGS WINDOW, NOT THE WORKSURFACE: do NOT mount the diagnostics panel as a standalone feature pane via pane_registry (the worksurface is being stripped to notes-only in MT-097). Instead render the panel widget (diagnostics/panel.rs) and host it as a SECTION inside the existing settings/options dialog (settings_dialog.rs), following the settings_editor_section.rs pattern - i.e. CREATE settings_diagnostics_section.rs as a new Settings section and register it so the operator opens it via Settings -> Diagnostics. It still PROJECTS the live internal_diagnostics state (snapshot_last_n + frame_stats + resource counters + heartbeat; typed empty Tier-3 Palmistry section until MT-093), with AccessKit metadata + a real wgpu screenshot proof of the Settings->Diagnostics section rendering live events. NOT a docked worksurface pane.

Follow editor_pane_factories.rs as the worked host-mount reference (MT-079): register the factory in the app factory HashMap keyed on the new PaneType::Diagnostics; the &self render reads the process-global diagnostics snapshot each frame — no shared-cell needed because the data is a process-global (MT-082), so the factory is stateless.
Adding PaneType::Diagnostics requires arms in BOTH the label() and default_label() match statements in pane_registry.rs (the compiler enforces exhaustiveness — do not miss one).
The panel is a pure projection (§5.8.4) — it MUST NOT cache its own authoritative copy of the diagnostics state; read the MT-082/005/006 globals each frame.
Map event_code (u16) -> a static human label for display; there is no free text to render (DiagEvent is typed integers) so the privacy invariant is preserved by construction.
Theme tokens only: no Color32 literal outside theme/palette.rs (CONTROL-4). Reuse the existing theme/syntax/palette layer like the other panes.
Screenshots ONLY to the external Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test/wp-kernel-015-mt-007/ root; git ls-files src/**/*.png must stay empty.

Read the KERNEL_BUILDER coder protocol under .GOV/ first; the Spec-Realism Gate forbids a panel that is mounted but not consumed by the live render loop (MT-079 RISK-2) — AC-007-1 is the live-mount proof, and visually inspect the screenshot (GLOBAL-INSPECT) before declaring the panel readable.
ports_from_react: app/src/components (the React diagnostics/flight-recorder views are reference for layout only; this is a native §10.12.5 three-tier panel, distinct from the existing flight_recorder_pane.rs)
binds_backend_api: NONE NEW. The panel reads in-process diagnostics globals only. The Tier-1 FR pane (flight_recorder_pane.rs) already exists separately; this panel does not re-implement it. Palmistry record forwarding is MT-093.

hbr_int_009_tier_obligations.reason: The panel surfaces Tier 2 internal_diagnostics; the Tier 1 FR has its own existing pane (flight_recorder_pane.rs). The three-tier §10.12.5 surface is rendered here, but this MT does not forward to or read the FR (that is MT-093's recovery-forward + the existing FR pane).

This MT IS the §5.8.4 in-app Diagnostics Panel — the live operator-facing consumer of internal_diagnostics (heartbeat, frame-time, resource counters, last-N events), host-mounted as a real pane and proven in the live app tree with a screenshot.

The panel renders the §10.12.5 Tier-3 Palmistry section (freeze/crash/debris records) with an honest empty-state placeholder; the live forwarding that fills it is MT-093. The three-tier layout obligation is satisfied (present, honestly empty) here.

risks: RISK-007-1: the panel is built as a widget but never host-mounted, so it never renders in the live app (MT-079 RISK-2 'mounted but not consumed'). Mitigate: AC-007-1 live-app mount proof + screenshot.
RISK-007-2: the panel caches its own copy of diagnostics state, drifting from the producer (violates §5.8.4 'projection, no own authority'). Mitigate: read the globals each frame; AC-007-2.
RISK-007-3: a Color32 literal breaks CONTROL-4. Mitigate: theme tokens only; AC-007-5 grep/test_theme.
RISK-007-4: a tracked PNG leaks into the repo (artifact hygiene). Mitigate: external-only screenshots; git ls-files src/**/*.png empty (AC-007-6).
RISK-007-5: missing a match arm when adding PaneType::Diagnostics breaks the build. Mitigate: add arms in both label() and default_label() (compiler-enforced exhaustiveness).
minimum_controls: MC-007-1: live-app mount + screenshot + visual inspection (AC-007-1/3).
MC-007-2: projection-only (reads globals each frame, no own authority) (AC-007-2).
MC-007-3: CONTROL-4 theme + no tracked PNG (AC-007-5/6).
MC-007-4: three-tier layout present with honest Tier-3 empty-state (AC-007-4).

research_provenance.field_stack: egui pane host-mount via the existing PaneFactory pattern (editor_pane_factories.rs / MT-079 worked reference); AccessKit metadata via the existing accessibility registry; theme-token rendering. The panel is a projection-only consumer (the spec's §5.8.4 producer/consumer split).
research_provenance.motivating_incident: After the 2026-06-26 freeze there was no first-party in-app surface to read the app's own liveness/frame/resource state; this panel is that surface (§5.8.4 / §10.12.5).
research_provenance.validation_plan: live-app mount + screenshot + projection-only + three-tier-layout + CONTROL-4; clippy -D warnings; full suite -j 2.

implementation_result.summary: Settings->Diagnostics panel (per operator steer - NOT a worksurface pane): DiagnosticsPanel widget + settings_diagnostics_section.rs hosted in settings_dialog.rs; projects live internal_diagnostics (heartbeat alive/uptime, frame p50/p95/slow, CPU/RSS/GPU adapter, last-N events) + honest Tier-3 Palmistry empty-state. AC-007-1..6 PASS; theme-token-only (CONTROL-4 clean); AccessKit ids; no spinner; operator-inspected screenshot legible no-overlap. GpuInfo strings stay panel-local (not in ring/Palmistry record).
implementation_result.visual_proof: Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-test/wp-kernel-015-mt-007/MT-087-diagnostics-settings-section-live.png - Settings->Diagnostics live (heartbeat 15 alive, frame p50 13.6ms, RSS 121.7MiB, GPU Dx12), backend OK.
implementation_result.note: full -j2 suite did not finish in the background window; no-regression via 1590 lib + 73 targeted neighbor tests green (per diagnostic_gate_policy).
